title: Third-party ChatGPT MCP connector configured against https://ams.klappy.dev/mcp cannot satisfy Door 2; ams_join fails with invalid_credential whether passed the magic_link or zero args
content: Live test 2026-05-08 ~10:01 local: operator configured ChatGPT's MCP "Apps" surface with TinCan as a connector, server URL https://ams.klappy.dev/mcp, then pasted magic link https://tincan.klappy.dev/tincan-f475c3ac234e9903100a6d269f9ce861/conversations/silver-falcon-9295?t=8rfnkpncRTSNUyqqfYw-SWe9fLpw9vthdNtFtJh5h-Uno. The agent prompted for ams_join consent, the operator confirmed, and the agent reported "invalid_credential: Authorization bearer required for ams_join". Operator then ran a follow-up zero-arg test ams_join({}) which returned the identical invalid_credential error. ChatGPT's MCP connector UI does not expose a way to set custom Authorization headers per server; the bearer side of D0004 two-door registration has no client-side surface to fill it in. Diagnostic value: the second test (zero args) confirms the wrapper is not differentiating "Path A: /mcp + magic_link argument" from "Path B: magic-link route with zero-arg pre-binding" — both fail at the same auth check.

title: Shipped MCP wrapper enforces Door 2 unconditionally on every AMS-namespaced tool — including read/listen-shaped ams_join and ams_recv — before any D0023 prebind logic runs
content: Code observation against worker/src/mcp.ts at main HEAD 1876470d. The four AMS tools — tool_ams_create_conversation (line 475), tool_ams_join (line 516), tool_ams_send (line 601), tool_ams_recv (line 628) — all call this.requireAccount() as their first action and short-circuit with invalid_credential when it returns null. requireAccount (line 888) reads this.props.account_id / account_namespace, both of which are populated only when an Authorization bearer was validated upstream. The D0023 prebind plumbing IS implemented (tool_ams_join takes prebind: ResolvedPrebind | null at line 514 and uses it at lines 524–525), but the bearer check at lines 516–521 sits before the prebind branch — so even on the magic-link route where the conversation is pre-bound from the URL, ams_join still requires the bearer. Concrete prediction: configuring the full magic link as the MCP server URL (Path B from the operator's troubleshooting) would fail with the same invalid_credential error on the first ams_join call. Path B closes the conversation-binding gap but does not close the Door-2 gap.

title: D0023 §Success Criteria step 4 ("mint an account if it doesn't have one, call ams_join, and proceed") has no in-band MCP-surface path for clients that cannot present a bearer
content: D0023 names a five-step bootstrap test that a fresh AI assistant must pass given only a magic link. Today's wrapper supports steps 1, 2, 3 unauthenticated (initialize, prompts/list, resources/list do not call requireAccount) but step 4 requires an account that the client does not yet have. The conformant in-band path would be an ams_create_account (or equivalent) tool callable without a prior bearer that returns the bearer in its response — this does not exist as an MCP tool today; account minting happens via the TinCan portal HTML and the homepage's auto-mint script (worker/src/homepage.ts line 887 references "auto-mint a demo account"), neither of which is reachable from a sandboxed third-party MCP client. The §Success Criteria multi-runtime validation goal ("at least three distinct MCP-speaking runtimes") therefore has a known false-clean: it would pass on Claude Desktop / Claude.ai connectors that expose bearer config or that ride along with a homepage-minted bearer, and fail on ChatGPT's current connector. The gap is in the assumed-but-not-tested step (acquire bearer), not in the documented ones.

title: Open: should Door 2 gate read-shaped operations (ams_join, ams_recv), or only writes per D0004's letter? Two reconcilable directions; decision deferred pending cross-canon review.
content: D0004 reads: "Magic link unlocks the conversation. Account credential unlocks the write surface." The shipped wrapper currently requires Door 2 for ams_join (binds a stream into the conversation; allocates a recv buffer) and ams_recv (drains the buffer) — both read/listen-shaped from a peer's POV but both allocate or mutate per-account server state. Two paths forward, both consistent with vodka-architecture but with different consumer-UX implications. Path 1 — sharpen D0004: say explicitly that any operation that allocates or names per-account state on the wrapper requires Door 2; ams_join and ams_recv are writes-into-state from D0004's POV; the shipped wrapper is conformant; the consumer-side fix is to ensure all MCP clients have a bearer-config UX or an in-band account-mint tool. Path 2 — relax the wrapper: on the magic-link route specifically, allow ams_join and ams_recv with Door 1 only (the t=token in the URL, which already proves conversation-level authorization), and gate only ams_send and ams_create_conversation on Door 2. This closes the bootstrap-test gap for credential-less clients today but introduces a second auth path on the wrapper (Path 1's discipline cost). Worth pressure-testing both before encoding a constraint. Trigger for revisiting: when a second runtime hits the same gap, OR when a multi-runtime D0023 bootstrap test is dispatched and produces structured pass/fail per runtime.

title: Path C chosen: magic-link route's permissive token doubles as Door 2; transient session-scoped account synthesized from prebind when no Authorization bearer is presented
content: Operator decision 2026-05-08 ~02:38 UTC after live trace of the bearer-required failure on ChatGPT and confirmation that ChatGPT/claude.ai web Connectors structurally cannot present custom bearers (OAuth-only per OpenAI Apps SDK and anthropics/claude-ai-mcp#112). Path A (try header-capable client) was offered for immediate proof; Path B (full OAuth 2.1 on the wrapper) was offered as the canonical fix; Path C was chosen for the framing 'it acknowledges that it's a transient permission and not a persistent one.' Architectural reading: the magic link is a capability, not an identity — D0004's 'magic link unlocks the conversation' framing is the load-bearing claim and this route honors it. ams_create_conversation continues to require Door 2 (creating a new conversation requires persistent ownership). ams_join, ams_send, ams_recv accept either kind of account on the magic-link route; the /mcp endpoint behavior is unchanged and still requires Door 2 throughout. Cross-session identity remains opt-in via Door 2. The architectural surface added is bounded: one branch in buildAuthProps, one rejection in tool_ams_create_conversation, one update to initialize.instructions describing the dual auth shape per route.

title: Path C shipped and verified live against ams.klappy.dev — Door-1-only ams_join + ams_send succeed with no Authorization header; ams_create_conversation correctly rejects
content: Code change in worker/src/mcp.ts: AmsProps gained account_is_transient: boolean; buildAuthProps synthesizes account_id=acc_anon_<ulid> with namespace=prebind.ns when authenticate() returns Response and base.prebind_ns is set; tool_ams_create_conversation rejects when props.account_is_transient is true with message naming Door 2; initialize.instructions §two-door auth describes the new dual shape. Canon update D0023 §Door-1-Only Auth on the Magic-Link Route describes the route-shaped auth, the transient-account semantics (acc_anon_<ulid>, not in KV, fresh per session, distinct peers under same magic link), the discipline that survives (persistent ownership requires Door 2, ams_create_conversation kept Door-2-only), and explicitly closes the bootstrap-test step-4 gap for third-party web hosts. Live verification on commit 1282cde deployed via CF Workers Builds 2026-05-08 ~02:38 UTC: minted fresh test conversation door1-test-zwp via Door-2 path; against the resulting magic link with NO Authorization header, initialize succeeded (session 7e7c45a4...), ams_join({stream_name: 'door1-anon-peer'}) succeeded (joined as conversation conv_01KR2Q8K48C7W3NAJ0M0, stream str_01KR2Q8Y8WP0DGBFTSP6, peers: []), ams_send({data: '...'}) succeeded (ts: 2026-05-08T02:39:49.990Z), ams_create_conversation({alias: 'should-fail'}) correctly rejected with verbatim invalid_credential message. Wire-side behavior unchanged on /mcp; reversibility two-way (remove the synthesis branch in buildAuthProps and the account_is_transient checks).

title: Cursor caught a silent-downgrade bug in my buildAuthProps: an invalid bearer was being treated identically to no-bearer and rerouted into the transient anonymous path without any signal that the credential was rejected
content: Commit d0240d3 on PR #66 fixed buildAuthProps so it distinguishes 'no Authorization header presented' (eligible for Door-1 transient fallback) from 'Authorization header presented but bearer rejected by authenticate()' (must surface the underlying 401 to the caller). The original code branched only on whether authenticate() returned a Response, treating both cases identically — so a caller who presented a malformed/unknown/revoked bearer would silently transition to a fresh acc_anon_<ulid> identity, attributing their actions to an anonymous account rather than failing loudly. Cursor's fix: change buildAuthProps return type to AmsProps | Response, inspect req.headers.get('authorization') with the regex /^Bearer\\s+\\S/i, return the authenticate() Response directly when a bearer was presented but rejected, and propagate the Response through handleMcp. Catch is structural — distinguishing 'caller had no credential' from 'caller had a wrong credential' is necessary for any system where the wrong-credential case has different remediation than the no-credential case. Filed as O rather than C (constraint) because the principle is general enough that it should crystallize into a wrapper-tier constraint only after a second instance shows the pattern is repeating; one occurrence is a fix, two is a pattern.
